channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Israel extends evacuation warnings in Lebanon, signaling a wider offensive
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Israeli military on Thursday warned people to evacuate communities in southern Lebanon that are outside a U.N.-declared buffer zone, signaling that it may widen a ground operation launched earlier this week against the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: FADI TAWIL and JACK JEFFERY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: North Korea's Kim threatens to destroy South Korea with nuclear strikes if provoked
item.description: North Korea says leader Kim Jong Un has threatened to use nuclear weapons and destroy South Korea permanently if provoked.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim And Kim Tong-Hyung, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: G20 environment ministers back funding for forest conservation
item.description: SAO PAULO (AP) — Environment ministers of the Group of 20 nations agreed Thursday to support the creation of funding sources for ecosystem services, acknowledging Brazil's proposal to establish a trust fund for forest conservation.
item.dc:creator: GABRIELA SÁ PESSOA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 6 migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras die near Guatemalan border after Mexican soldiers open fire
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexican army troops opened fire on a truck carrying migrants from a half dozen countries, and six migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras died in an event that President Claudia Sheinbaum described Thursday as “deplorable.
item.dc:creator: MARK STEVENSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Justice Department takes action against Russian hacking group
item.description: U.S. officials have seized dozens of web domains used by Russian intelligence agents and their proxies to steal Americans’ information, the Justice Department announced Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: South Africa's top court will decide if impeachment proceedings against the president can be revived
item.description: CAPE TOWN, South Africa (AP) — South Africa's Constitutional Court said Thursday that it would hear a case next month brought by two opposition parties to revive impeachment proceedings against President Cyril Ramaphosa over a scandal involving more than $500,000 in cash that was hidden in a couch a
item.dc:creator: GERALD IMRAY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 6 migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras die near Guatemalan border after Mexican soldiers open fire
item.description: Mexican army troops opened fire on a truck carrying migrants from a half dozen countries, and six migrants from Egypt, Peru and Honduras died in an event that President Claudia Sheinbaum called “deplorable.”.
item.dc:creator: Mark Stevenson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Dozens of migrants still missing off Djibouti's coast after smugglers forced them out of boats
item.description: MOGADISHU, Somalia (AP) — Rescuers searched Thursday for dozens of migrants from Africa still missing after smugglers apparently forced them out of two boats in the Red Sea off the coast of Djibouti earlier this week, the Djiboutian coast guard said.
item.dc:creator: OMAR FARUK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: The Harpole Treasure includes one of the most valuable pieces of ancient jewelry found in Britain
item.description: The Harpole Treasure was discovered in 2022, and refers to a collection of valuable relics unearthed from a burial ground in England. Among the finds was a unique piece of jewelry.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Belarus opposition urges immediate release of over 200 political prisoners in dire state
item.description: TALLINN, Estonia (AP) — Belarusian opposition leader in exile Sviatlana Tsikhanouskaya on Thursday said 224 political prisoners in Belarus must be freed urgently, and called on Western countries to negotiate their release with the Belarusian authorities.
item.dc:creator: YURAS KARMANAU, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UK gives sovereignty of the long-contested Chagos Islands to Mauritius. A key US base remains
item.description: LONDON (AP) — The British government agreed Thursday to hand sovereignty of the long-contested Chagos Islands, an archipelago of more than 60 islands in the Indian Ocean, to Mauritius, in a deal to secure the future of a strategically important U.K.-U.S. military base.
item.dc:creator: PAN PYLAS, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Victor Perahia, Holocaust survivor and president of French Union of Auschwitz Deportees, dies at 91
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Victor Perahia, the president of the French Union of Auschwitz Deportees and a Holocaust survivor, has died. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least 78 people died when a boat with 278 on board capsized in eastern Congo, official says
item.description: A Congolese governor says at least 78 people have died when an overcrowded boat with 278 passengers capsized on Lake Kivu in eastern Congo.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Alonga And Jean-Yves Kamale, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: New NATO chief Mark Rutte visits Ukraine in his first trip since taking office
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — New NATO Secretary-General Mark Rutte visited Ukraine on Thursday in his first official trip since taking office and pledging the alliance's continued support for Kyiv in its war with Russia.
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA and SAMYA KULLAB, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Typhoon Krathon makes landfall in Taiwan, packing fierce winds and torrential rain
item.description: KAOHSIUNG, Taiwan (AP) — Typhoon Krathon made landfall Thursday in Taiwan's major port city of Kaohsiung, bringing torrential rains and fierce winds to the island's south. Trees were brought down by high winds and roads were flooded, prompting the closure of schools and businesses.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan hospital fire leaves at least 9 dead as typhoon batters island's south
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — A fire at a hospital in southern Taiwan killed at least nine people Thursday as the island was being battered by a typhoon.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Two boats carrying migrants sink in the Red Sea off Djibouti's coast killing 45, UN says
item.description: DJIBOUTI (AP) — Two vessels carrying migrants from Africa sank in the Red Sea off the coast of Djibouti, killing 45 people, the U.N. migration agency said Tuesday. The boats had departed from Yemen carrying 310 people, the International Organization for Migration said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former Singapore minister sentenced to a year in prison for receiving illegal gifts
item.description: KUALA LUMPUR, Malaysia (AP) — A former Singaporean Cabinet minister was sentenced Thursday to a year in prison after he pleaded guilty to charges of receiving illegal gifts, in a rare criminal case involving a minister in the Asian financial hub. Former Transport Minister S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: This German church is the tallest in the world. Until Spain's La Sagrada Familia is done, anyway
item.description: ULM, Germany (AP) — The Ulmer Münster in southern Germany is the world's tallest church. For now, anyway. The Gothic-style Lutheran church's reign — begun in May 31, 1890 — could end in 2025, when La Sagrada Familia Basilica's “Tower of Jesus Christ” in Spain is set to be completed.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO and PIETRO DE CRISTOFARO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum keeps doing morning briefings, though her style is unlike her predecessor's
item.description: MEXICO CITY (AP) — Mexico's new President Claudia Sheinbaum started her day Wednesday much like her political mentor, ex-President Andrés Manuel López Obrador, began most of his: with an early morning news briefing from the National Palace.
item.dc:creator: FABIOLA SÁNCHEZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel presses forward on two fronts and reports 8 combat deaths as fears of a wider war mount
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel pressed forward on two fronts Wednesday, pursuing a ground incursion into Lebanon against Hezbollah that left eight Israeli soldiers dead and conducting strikes in Gaza that killed dozens, including children.
item.dc:creator: KAREEM CHEHAYEB and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: World War II-era bomb explodes at busy international airport, cratering taxiway
item.description: An American bomb from World War II buried beneath a Japanese airport exploded Wednesday, cratering a taxiway and forcing the cancellation of dozens of flights.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: WikiLeaks’ Julian Assange says he pleaded ‘guilty to journalism’ in order to be freed
item.description: In Julian Assange's first remarks since his release from prison in June, the WikiLeaks founder said he was freed after 5 years of incarceration because he 'pled guilty to journalism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Rwanda records at least 9 deaths from Marburg outbreak
item.description: Rwanda has declared a public outbreak of the Marburg virus, confirming nearly 30 infections and at least nine deaths as of the end of September.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, The Hill

item.title: Bolton says Israel should destroy Iran's nuclear weapons program
item.description: Former U.S. Ambassador to the United Nations John Bolton said Israel should “destroy” Iran’s nuclear weapons program during a Wednesday appearance on NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Putin signs law granting immunity to criminal defendants who join the army
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin signed a law Wednesday that will allow criminal defendants to skip prosecution and potential prison time if they enlist in the army.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Biden opposes Israeli attack on Iranian nuclear sites
item.description: President Biden said Wednesday that he does not support Israel carrying out strikes on Iranian nuclear sites as the U.S. and its allies mull additional sanctions against Tehran.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Prince Harry visits the small mountain kingdom of Lesotho, where he's called 'the warrior'
item.description: MASERU, Lesotho (AP) — Prince Harry is visiting the small mountain kingdom of Lesotho having co-founded a youth charity there in 2006 in honor of his late mother, Princess Diana.
item.dc:creator: KEKETSO PHAKELA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel to have 'substantial response' to Iran attacks: John Bolton
item.description: Iran launched at least 180 missiles into Israel on Tuesday, less than a day after Israel began ground attacks on the militant group Hezbollah in Lebanon.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: What Iran attacks mean for Americans in Israel: Vittert
item.description: NewsNation's Leland Vittert believes there will not be immediate panic for most of the more than 500,000 Americans living in Israel.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Israel under attack: 2 gunmen kill 6 civilians, injure others near Tel Aviv
item.description: A mass shooting by at least two individuals near Tel Aviv has killed and injured multiple people — an incident authorities believe is terrorism.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Iran preparing imminent missile attack on Israel, US warns, pledging 'severe consequences'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Iran is preparing to “imminently” launch a ballistic missile attack on Israel, according to a senior U.S. administration official, who warned Tuesday of “severe consequences” should it take place.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI, JULIA FRANKEL and BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Israeli raids against Hezbollah signal a wider ground incursion into Lebanon could come soon
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — The Israeli military launched small ground raids against Hezbollah and sealed off communities along its northern border on Monday as Israeli artillery pounded southern Lebanon and signals grew that more forces could soon be sent across the border to fight the Iran-backed militants.
item.dc:creator: MATTHEW LEE, SAMY MAGDY, ABBY SEWELL and NATALIE MELZER Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hezbollah confirms its leader Hassan Nasrallah was killed in an Israeli airstrike
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Lebanon’s Hezbollah group confirmed on Saturday that its leader and one of its founding members, Hassan Nasrallah, was killed in an Israeli airstrike in a southern suburb of Beirut.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Netanyahu vows to use 'full force' against Hezbollah and dims hopes for a cease-fire
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu on Thursday vowed to carry out “full force” strikes against Hezbollah until it ceases firing rockets across the border, dimming hopes for a cease-fire proposal put forth by U.S. and European officials.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, BASSEM MROUE and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, NewsNation
